title: Prevalence of rotator cuff tears in the general population: a systematic review
journal: Journal of Shoulder and Elbow Surgery
abstract: Purpose: To determine the prevalence of rotator cuff tears. Methods: Systematic review of population-based studies. Results: Overall prevalence was 20.7% (95% CI 16.5-25.4). Prevalence increased with age, reaching 50% in those over 80 years.

title: Diabetes mellitus and rotator cuff repair outcomes: a meta-analysis
abstract: Purpose: To analyze the impact of diabetes on rotator cuff repair outcomes. Methods: Meta-analysis of 14 studies (3,892 patients). Results: Diabetic patients had higher re-tear rates (OR 2.34, 95% CI 1.75-3.14) and worse functional scores. HbA1c >7% was associated with poorer healing.

title: Conference abstract: preliminary results of a new suture anchor technique for rotator cuff repair
Smoking and rotator cuff disease: a systematic review and meta-analysis
abstract: Purpose: To evaluate the association between smoking and rotator cuff disease. Methods: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 18 studies. Results: Current smokers had 1.86-fold higher risk of rotator cuff tears (OR 1.86, 95% CI 1.39-2.50) and 2.1-fold higher re-tear rates after repair.

title: Obesity and shoulder disorders: an umbrella review
abstract: Purpose: Umbrella review of the relationship between obesity and shoulder disorders including rotator cuff disease. Methods: Included 8 systematic reviews and meta-analyses. Results: BMI >30 was associated with increased rotator cuff tear prevalence (OR 1.73) and worse surgical outcomes.
